# @vllnt/convex-metering

> Metered usage records — idempotent usage events rolled up per billing period, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-metering` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0`. It stores
three sandboxed tables — `meters` (the definition: an `aggregation` rule of `sum`/`max`/`last` plus a
display `unit`, unique per `(scope, key)`), `records` (append-only raw usage events, the audit trail
and idempotency source), and `rollups` (the materialized per-`(meter, subject, period)` aggregate,
the O(1) read for billing). `defineMeter` registers a meter; `record` writes a usage event, folds it
into the period rollup per the meter's aggregation, and is idempotent when given an `idempotencyKey`
(a retry returns `{ recorded: false, reason: "duplicate" }`). `usage` and `listUsage` read rollups;
`reset` clears one `(meter, subject, period)`; `pruneRecords` deletes raw records older than a
required cutoff while leaving rollups — the billable truth — intact. `period` is an opaque host
string (the component never parses dates); time is server-sourced. The component owns accurate,
idempotent usage accounting; the host owns the meter meaning, the opaque `subjectRef`, the period
bucketing, pricing (billed in the host's own tables), retention, and auth.

## Examples

- [Example component wrapper](example/convex/example.ts): Host-app wrapper functions
- [Example tests](example/convex/example.test.ts): End-to-end usage coverage with `convex-test`
